methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 11 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 22.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Credo Semiconductor
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $5B+

private.name: Ouster
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $1.5B

data.narrative:
Aeva Technologies is a public-stage company in Chips & Semiconductors that has raised $660M+ and currently trades at a $1B+ valuation. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 11 companies in the same sector and similar valuation tier, spanning 3 public comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers including Etched, SambaNova Systems, Credo Semiconductor, Tenstorrent, and Rebellions.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach here. Across the 11-company peer universe, the median valuation stands at $2.0B, with a range spanning $500M at the low end to $5.0B at the high end. This method anchors directly on observed market valuations for direct competitors at similar stages and scales. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (median 22.3x across the three public semiconductors comparables) cannot be applied, as Aeva's revenue figures are not available on file.

The triangulated range supported by available data is $1.7B to $2.3B, with a median point estimate of $2.0B. This implies upside relative to the company's stated $1B+ current valuation, though the analysis is constrained by reliance on a single applicable method. Key uncertainties include revenue scale and growth trajectory relative to peers, which would be material inputs to a fuller valuation framework. The peer-median approach provides a floor of conviction but should be supplemented with additional financial data to reduce estimation risk.
